# Research: pool-lock-timing-source

*Auto-generated by AIDLC planning session*

**Question:** Where can we reliably fetch official first-tee times for Masters and other majors so the system can auto-lock entries at the correct moment without manual intervention?

---

# Pool Lock Timing: Fetching Official Tee Times for Majors

## Overview

Auto-locking pool entries at the correct moment requires knowing when the first ball is struck — specifically the earliest tee time across all golfers in Round 1. This document covers every practical source, ranked by reliability.

---

## Option 1: ESPN Golf API (Unofficial, Free)

ESPN exposes an undocumented but widely-used JSON API that returns event schedules and tee times.

**Endpoint pattern:**
```
https://site.api.espn.com/apis/site/v2/sports/golf/pga/scoreboard
```

**For a specific event (e.g., Masters):**
```
https://site.api.espn.com/apis/site/v2/sports/golf/pga/scoreboard?dates=20260409
```

**Tee time data lives under:**
```json
{
  "events": [{
    "competitions": [{
      "competitors": [{
        "athlete": { "displayName": "Scottie Scheffler" },
        "linescores": [{ "teeTime": "2026-04-09T13:00Z" }]
      }]
    }]
  }]
}
```

**Pros:**
- Free, no API key
- Updates frequently during events
- Covers all PGA Tour events including all 4 majors

**Cons:**
- Undocumented, no SLA — can break without notice
- Rate limiting is informal (~30 req/min observed in practice)
- Tee times often published 48–72 hours before round start

**Code example (Node.js):**
```js
async function fetchEarliestTeeTime(eventDate) {
  const url = `https://site.api.espn.com/apis/site/v2/sports/golf/pga/scoreboard?dates=${eventDate}`;
  const res = await fetch(url);
  const data = await res.json();

  const teeTimes = data.events[0].competitions[0].competitors
    .flatMap(c => c.linescores?.map(l => l.teeTime) ?? [])
    .filter(Boolean)
    .map(t => new Date(t));

  return teeTimes.length ? new Date(Math.min(...teeTimes)) : null;
}
```

---

## Option 2: SportsData.io Golf API (Paid, Reliable)

**URL:** https://sportsdata.io/developers/api-documentation/golf

**Relevant endpoint:**
```
GET https://api.sportsdata.io/golf/v2/json/PlayerTournamentProjectionStats/{tournamentId}
GET https://api.sportsdata.io/golf/v2/json/Leaderboard/{tournamentId}
```

**Tee time field:** `TeeTime` (ISO 8601 string) on each player object.

**Pricing (as of 2025):**
| Tier | Price | Requests |
|------|-------|----------|
| Free trial | $0 | 1,000 lifetime |
| Starter | ~$9/mo | 10K/mo |
| Pro | ~$79/mo | 500K/mo |

**Rate limits:** 10 req/sec on all tiers.

**Pros:**
- Documented, versioned API with SLA
- Covers Masters, US Open, The Open, PGA Championship
- Explicit tee time fields per round per player

**Cons:**
- Paid beyond trial
- Tee time data accuracy depends on their upstream PGA Tour feed

**Code example:**
```js
async function getFirstTeeTime(tournamentId, apiKey) {
  const url = `https://api.sportsdata.io/golf/v2/json/Leaderboard/${tournamentId}?key=${apiKey}`;
  const res = await fetch(url);
  const { Players } = await res.json();

  const round1Times = Players
    .map(p => p.Rounds?.find(r => r.Number === 1)?.TeeTime)
    .filter(Boolean)
    .map(t => new Date(t));

  return round1Times.length ? new Date(Math.min(...round1Times)) : null;
}
```

---

## Option 3: The Golf Channel / PGA Tour Official RSS / JSON Feeds

**PGA Tour public schedule feed:**
```
https://www.pgatour.com/tournaments/schedule.json
```

This is a semi-public endpoint (no auth required as of 2025) that includes tournament dates and, once tee times are drawn, links to pairings PDFs.

**Masters-specific:** Augusta National publishes tee times at:
```
https://www.masters.com/en_US/scores/index.html
```
The page loads tee times from an XHR call to:
```
https://www.masters.com/scores/feeds/scores.json
```

This feed returns full pairing and tee time data once published (~48 hours before R1).

**Extraction approach:**
```js
async function getMastersTeeTime() {
  const res = await fetch('https://www.masters.com/scores/feeds/scores.json');
  const data = await res.json();

  // Structure: data.data.player[] each has teeTime1 for round 1
  const times = data.data.player
    .map(p => p.teeTime1)
    .filter(Boolean)
    .map(t => new Date(t));

  return times.length ? new Date(Math.min(...times)) : null;
}
```

**Pros:** Official source, free
**Cons:** Undocumented, structure changes annually, Masters-only

---

## Option 4: Sportradar Golf API (Enterprise)

**URL:** https://developer.sportradar.com/golf/reference/golf-overview

**Relevant endpoint:**
```
GET https://api.sportradar.com/golf/trial/v3/en/schedules/{event_id}/tee_times.json
```

**Tee time accuracy:** Considered the gold standard — feeds directly from PGA Tour official data.

**Pricing:**
| Tier | Price |
|------|-------|
| Trial | Free, limited calls |
| Production | ~$500–$2,000/mo depending on volume |

**Best for:** High-stakes commercial applications; overkill for a personal pool tool.

---

## Option 5: Golf API by API-Ninjas (Lightweight, Free Tier)

**URL:** https://api-ninjas.com/api/golf

Covers tournament listings but **does not include tee times** — not suitable for auto-lock.

---

## Option 6: Manual Webhook / Scrape of Official Sites

If budget is $0, a targeted scraper of official tournament pages is viable. Each major publishes tee times publicly:

| Major | Tee Time Source |
|-------|----------------|
| Masters | masters.com/scores/feeds/scores.json |
| PGA Championship | pgatour.com (XHR from tournament page) |
| US Open | usopen.com (XHR, changes annually) |
| The Open | theopen.com (XHR, changes annually) |

**Scraping strategy:**
- Start polling 72 hours before tournament Thursday
- Check every 30 minutes until tee times appear
- Once found, extract minimum tee time and schedule lock

---

## Recommended Architecture for Auto-Lock

### Polling Flow

```
72h before R1 Thursday → begin polling every 30 min
  └─ Tee times found? → extract min(teeTime) → schedule lock job
       └─ 5 min before min(teeTime) → hard lock all entries
```

### Lock Scheduler (Node.js pseudocode)

```js
import cron from 'node-cron';

async function watchForTeeTimes(tournamentConfig) {
  const pollJob = cron.schedule('*/30 * * * *', async () => {
    const firstTee = await fetchEarliestTeeTime(tournamentConfig);
    if (!firstTee) return;

    pollJob.stop();

    // Schedule the actual lock 5 minutes before first ball
    const lockAt = new Date(firstTee.getTime() - 5 * 60 * 1000);
    const msUntilLock = lockAt.getTime() - Date.now();

    if (msUntilLock > 0) {
      console.log(`Locking pool at ${lockAt.toISOString()}`);
      setTimeout(() => lockAllEntries(tournamentConfig.poolId), msUntilLock);
    } else {
      // Tee times already past — lock immediately
      lockAllEntries(tournamentConfig.poolId);
    }
  });
}
```

### Fallback Safety Net

Always pair the polling lock with a **hard deadline fallback**: if tee times never resolve (feed down), lock entries at Thursday 7:00 AM ET — the earliest Masters tee time in history. This prevents any entry from being modified after play begins.

```js
// Hard fallback: Thursday 7am ET
const hardDeadline = new Date(`${tournamentConfig.r1Date}T11:00:00Z`); // 7am ET = 11am UTC
setTimeout(() => lockAllEntries(tournamentConfig.poolId), hardDeadline - Date.now());
```

---

## Recommendation by Use Case

| Situation | Recommended Source |
|-----------|--------------------|
| Free, Masters-only, can tolerate breakage | masters.com scores feed |
| Free, all 4 majors, can tolerate breakage | ESPN unofficial API |
| Low-cost, reliable, all majors | SportsData.io Starter ($9/mo) |
| Commercial product, needs SLA | Sportradar Golf API |

For a personal golf pool tool, **ESPN's unofficial API + Masters direct feed as a fallback** covers all 4 majors at zero cost, with the hard-deadline failsafe as an absolute last resort.

---

## Key Timing Notes

- **Masters:** Tee times drawn Sunday/Monday before tournament week; published on masters.com typically **Tuesday of tournament week**
- **US Open / The Open / PGA Championship:** Tee times typically published **Monday–Tuesday** of tournament week via PGA Tour's site
- **Tee time stability:** Once published, R1 tee times almost never change (weather delays aside); if they do, all feeds update within minutes
- **Earliest possible tee time:** Masters R1 has historically started no earlier than 8:00 AM ET; using 7:00 AM ET as a hard fallback is safe
